generated_text: The Project Manager disapproved of the intelligent controller concept because it involved using speech recognition and gesture recognition technologies, which were deemed risky and potentially unreliable. The Project Manager was concerned about the reliability of these technologies and the potential for issues like broken arms or limited vocabulary in speech recognition. Additionally, the Project Manager noted that the intelligent controller would require cooperation with a research institute, which was not considered cost-effective or necessary for the project.
expected_answer: The project manager put forward that the speech recognition wouldn't work when several persons wanted to change the channel at the same time, and the cost would surely rise if adding the two technologies. In response to that, the user interface designer said they would just cooperate with some research institute and the vocabulary was limited, so it wouldn't cost much. Then the project manager posed the language problem. The user interface designer answered they would have some language adaptations so the product could be used by people from all countries. Finally, there were still problems with the microphone and camera that were necessary for the two technologies.

generated_text: The discussion focused on the effect of training neural networks on different languages. PhD D and Professor B discussed training a network on TI-digits (English) and testing it on Italian digits, which resulted in poor performance. They also tested a network trained on a single language (Italian) on another language (Spanish), which showed improvement. Finally, a network trained on a three-language database (including Italian, Spanish, and another language) performed better than one trained only on task data. The key takeaway was that training on a broader language set improved performance across different languages.
expected_answer: Using multiple languages led to more diversity which potentially hindered the model performance. The professor was skeptical about discussing the results since they had not received any good ones so far.
